## TL;DR
The "semantic folding" risk you raised is real. I concede that TL;DR's reliability degrades after round 2,
and orchestration decisions must rely on the stance-label distribution rather than TL;DR text alone.
[stance: concede]

## Argument
Re-reading the concurrent path in `session_manager.py:create_session()`,
indeed a TL;DR that says "add a lock" cannot distinguish `threading.Lock` from `fcntl.flock` semantically.
Proposed fix: when orchestrating, the moderator must look at the stance-label distribution (extracted via grep) as the structured signal of convergence.
